title: Pure Core, UI Adapters, Dependency Direction, and Snapshots · Runtime Lens: Command Event Flow
summary: Cells call Canvas methods, algorithms read widgets, tests require a display, and mutable state leaks across attempts. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the command event flow increment in pure maze core with Tk adapter ports. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Translate input into commands, domain transitions into events, and events into adapter effects without cycles.
Publish immutable or copied snapshots so rendering and tests cannot mutate authoritative state accidentally.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-architecture-pure-core-2
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-architecture-pure-core-2.
# Case 7417: a keyboard-only puzzle tournament.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change the grid dimensions and preserve every invariant.
def worked_maze_architecture_pure_core_2(domain_modules: int = 7, ui_imports_in_core: int = 0, adapter_ports: int = 6, fake_adapter_cases: int = 18, snapshot_mutations: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "gridworked2",
        "topology7417",
        "search2dekrow",
        "eventworked27417",
        "access7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if domain_modules < 1 or adapter_ports < 4:
        return False, adapter_ports, "architecture-shape"
    if ui_imports_in_core > 0:
        return False, ui_imports_in_core, "dependency-inversion"
    if fake_adapter_cases < 8:
        return False, fake_adapter_cases, "adapter-evidence"
    if snapshot_mutations > 0:
        return False, snapshot_mutations, "snapshot-isolation"
    assert ui_imports_in_core == 0 and snapshot_mutations == 0
    return True, fake_adapter_cases, "pure-core-boundary"

# Evidence: maze-command-event-flow-1bzw3p
# Competency: maze-command-event-flow.
# Case 4046: a clean desktop release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: the prior good state must survive failure.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_command_event_flow_1bzw3p(domain_modules: int = 7, ui_imports_in_core: int = 0, adapter_ports: int = 6, fake_adapter_cases: int = 18, snapshot_mutations: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bzw3p = "1bzw3p-p3wzb1-4046"
    assert evidence_variant_1bzw3p
    evidence_axes_1bzw3p = (
        "grid1bzw3p",
        "topology4046",
        "searchp3wzb1",
        "event1bzw3p4046",
        "access40461bzw3p",
        "recoveryp3wzb14046",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bzw3p)) == 6
    if domain_modules < 1 or adapter_ports < 4:
        return False, adapter_ports, "architecture-shape"
    if ui_imports_in_core > 0:
        return False, ui_imports_in_core, "dependency-inversion"
    if fake_adapter_cases < 8:
        return False, fake_adapter_cases, "adapter-evidence"
    if snapshot_mutations > 0:
        return False, snapshot_mutations, "snapshot-isolation"
    assert ui_imports_in_core == 0 and snapshot_mutations == 0
    return True, fake_adapter_cases, "pure-core-boundary"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Translate input into commands, domain transitions into events, and events into adapter effects without cycles.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Command Event Flow model · Theory Command Event Flow, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Command Event Flow precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Command Event Flow model · Theory Command Event Flow, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 3726 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to enforce a pure domain core, narrow UI ports, immutable snapshots, and testable dependency direction; event-loop work must remain cancellable; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
